steps.text:
Welcome to Claudzooks!

You're about to learn how to use the terminal — a powerful, text-based way to control your computer. By the end, you'll build a real web page using an AI coding assistant.

The terminal is different from what you're used to. There's no mouse, no clicking, no dragging. Everything happens through the keyboard. The terminal was invented before the mouse existed. It's the most fundamental way to talk to a computer, and it's still how most professional software gets built.

Before we start, let’s make sure your computer is set up.

You’ll need Python installed for later lessons (6 and 7). Don’t worry about it now — we’ll check when the time comes.

Claude Code is an AI coding tool that runs in your terminal. You won't need it until Lesson 6, but here's how to install it later:

1. You need a Claude subscription (Pro, Max, or Team) at claude.ai
2. Open your terminal and run:
       curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
3. Restart your terminal (close and reopen it)
4. Run: claude
5. Follow the prompts to log in

Don't worry about Claude Code now. We'll check again before Lesson 6.

One more thing: after each lesson, feel free to open your terminal on your own and practice the commands you just learned. Repetition is how they stick.

Let's get started!
